headline: Federal Constitution amendment to enshrine right to housing proposed
context: A cross-party coalition of 34 MPs proposed a constitutional amendment to enshrine the right to adequate housing in the Federal Constitution in 2025.

cards.big: 34 MPs propose constitutional amendment to enshrine right to housing
cards.sub: Cross-party initiative targets Malaysia's 120,000 homeless population

cards.big: Federal Constitution does not include socio-economic rights such as housing
cards.sub: Proposed Article 5A would make adequate housing a justiciable right

cards.big: ICESCR Article 11 recognises right to adequate housing as component of living standard
cards.sub: Malaysia ratified ICESCR in 2022, creating obligation to progressively realise rights

cards.big: 1.2 million Malaysians live in inadequate housing per UN-Habitat 2024 assessment
cards.sub: B40 households spend average 42% of income on housing versus recommended 30%

cards.big: Without constitutional protection, housing remains a market commodity, not a human right
National Housing Department endorsed the proposal in principle in February 2025
